user_message: How should I coach David Chen on Blue Yonder Airlines? I met with them last week but can't remember the details.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-01 16:23 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Blue Yonder Airlines territory planning

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with territory planning. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
Date: 2024-02-02 13:16 EST
Subject: territory planning - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with territory planning. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-10 14:49 EST
Subject: Re: Re: territory planning Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with territory planning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Blue Yonder Airlines
Date: 2024-02-11
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines), David Chen (Microsoft), Michelle (Blue Yonder Airlines)

[00:02:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on territory planning from our last session.

[00:06:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:11:00] Michelle (Blue Yonder Airlines): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:14:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:18:00] Robert Singh (Blue Yonder Airlines): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:22:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-A trap (Produces multi-page brief (loses utility)); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-A failure mode (Produces multi-page brief (loses utility))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-A -- Produces multi-page brief (loses utility)
